title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone · Incident Case: Net Hardware Tools
summary: Use software, hardware, discovery, capture, and device evidence to design, implement, secure, operate, and repair a branch network. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net hardware tools increment in a performance-based branch network capstone. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose toner, cable tester, tap, Wi-Fi analyzer, and visual fault locator with correct media, safety, and evidence limits.
Use LLDP or CDP and speed tests while accounting for device support, advertisement trust, test path, server, and competing load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Build Net Hardware Tools evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-hardware-tools-1q0v8k" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Hardware Tools into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Net Hardware Tools model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Hardware Tools applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose toner, cable tester, tap, Wi-Fi analyzer, and visual fault locator with correct media, safety, and evidence limits.
